title: Easy DIY Floor-Standing Upholstered Headboard
summary: A padded, fabric-wrapped headboard on a 2x4 frame — batting and fabric stapled around the back, sized here for a queen bed with the same construction scaling to any width.
description:
This headboard is a plain 2x4 rectangle first, and everything that makes it look upholstered — batting, padding, and a fabric wrap — comes after the frame and panel are already structurally complete. The frame's OUTSIDE dimensions are the finished headboard's dimensions, which is worth confirming against your actual bed and wall space before cutting a single board, since there's no trimming a frame down after assembly.

Cleats for attaching the headboard to a bed frame or footboard get added to the back of the panel next, positioned to match wherever your specific bed frame needs the attachment points — this varies enough between bed frames that cut-to-size is the right instruction rather than a fixed number.

The padding and fabric step is genuinely simple despite looking like upholstery work: layer batting or old comforters over the panel, staple neatly at the back, then cover in fabric and staple again, pulling snug as you go to avoid wrinkles on the front face. **This headboard is floor-standing, not free-standing** — it must be attached either to the wall with a French cleat or to the bed frame itself for stability, since a padded panel this size and this top-heavy will tip forward if left resting unsecured against a wall.

materials.name: 2x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Sized here for a queen (60" wide, 48" tall) headboard frame.

materials.name: Wood glue (if using nails or staples)

cutList.note: Up to 96" for a wider headboard — the outside frame dimension is the finished headboard size.
Desired height minus 3" — up to 45" for a 48"-tall headboard.
Cut to size and positioned to match your specific bed frame or footboard.
Cut to match the frame's outside dimensions.

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.body: Build the 2x4 frame for the headboard — the outside dimensions of the frame are your completed headboard's dimensions, so confirm they match your bed and wall space before cutting.

steps.title: Add the attachment cleats
steps.body: Add the cleats in the position needed to attach to your specific bed frame or footboard, cut to size for your hardware.

steps.title: Attach the panel
steps.body: Attach the plywood or paneling to the frame, with the cleats directly against the back side of the panel.
materials: Wood glue (if using nails or staples)

steps.title: Pad and cover the headboard
steps.body: Cover the front of the headboard with batting, padding, or old comforters, stapling neatly at the back. Then cover in your chosen fabric, pulling snug as you go to avoid wrinkles on the front, and staple at the back.

steps.title: Attach for stability
steps.body: Attach the finished headboard to the wall using a French cleat, or to the bed frame itself, for stability — this headboard must not be left free-standing against a wall.
